frameworks.name: law of least mental effort
frameworks.one_line: brain minimizes cognitive demand when choosing between tasks

frameworks.name: transdiagnostic latent-factor psychopathology
frameworks.one_line: symptom dimensions cut across categorical diagnoses

core_question: Which clinically-relevant symptoms, traits, and transdiagnostic factors are associated with avoidance of mental effort across individuals?
key_assumptions: effort avoidance is a measurable individual-difference trait
psychiatric constructs are better modeled dimensionally than categorically
single-diagnosis findings should generalize to transdiagnostic structure
manipulations: diverse battery of clinical symptom/trait measures across one large sample
extraction of transdiagnostic latent factors from the symptom battery
key_findings: lack of perseverance (impulsiveness dimension) was associated with greater mental effort avoidance
positive urgency, distress intolerance, obsessive-compulsive symptoms, and disordered eating were associated with less effort avoidance
a latent factor of compulsive behavior and intrusive thoughts also predicted less effort avoidance
interpretations: deviations from normative effort expenditure span multiple psychiatric constructs rather than mapping to one diagnosis
effort avoidance does not align in one direction with pathology — some constructs increase and others decrease it
